response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack engineer with a long career, but his core expertise is overwhelmingly in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — not the Java/Kotlin/JVM stack this role demands. Java appears only peripherally (robotic arms, some tooling at Altom), Kotlin is absent entirely, and there is no mention of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise JVM framework. His one IDE-adjacent experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) was C/C++ based, not JVM tooling. While he shows genuine interest in developer tooling and software craft, the technical stack mismatch is fundamental and not bridgeable for a mid-senior JVM IDE specialist role.
strengths: Demonstrated interest in developer tooling and IDE internals through NI LabWindows/CVI work and open-source test framework projects (Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
Strong software craftsmanship practices — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, Code Kata sessions — align with the quality and reliability standards the role requires
Experience with large-scale, high-throughput systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic) shows ability to reason about performance and complex codebases
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only marginal Java usage; primary expertise is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, which are misaligned with the role's core JVM requirement
Zero mention of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise Java framework — the role's central technical domain is entirely absent from the resume
IDE internals experience is limited to a C-based IDE (LabWindows/CVI) from a brief early-career role; no IntelliJ Platform, LSP, or JVM language tooling work is evident

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise JVM frameworks
